context: Tests whether the agent runs tessl plugin lint after applying fixes, checks for token cost increases, and recommends moving heavy content to docs (on-demand) rather than rules (always loaded) when front-loaded tokens balloon. This covers the Phase 3.3 lint-after-each-fix instruction from optimize-skill-performance.

checklist.name: tessl plugin lint command used
checklist.description: Script uses tessl plugin lint as the command for plugin validation (not tessl lint plugin, tessl validate, or other variants)

checklist.name: Plugin path argument provided
checklist.description: Script passes the plugin path (./database-migrator/ or equivalent) as an argument to tessl plugin lint

checklist.name: Lint run after each change set
checklist.description: Script runs tessl plugin lint at least twice — once after each distinct set of changes (i.e., there is more than one lint invocation, not a single final lint)

checklist.name: Token cost ballooning flagged
checklist.description: Script or notes include logic or commentary that flags when front-loaded token costs increase significantly — not just that lint passes or fails

checklist.name: Move to docs recommended
checklist.description: validation-notes.md recommends moving heavy content to docs (or equivalent on-demand storage) when front-loaded tokens increase significantly

checklist.name: Docs vs rules distinction
checklist.description: validation-notes.md explicitly distinguishes between docs (loaded on-demand) and rules (always loaded) — and states that heavy content should go to docs, not rules

checklist.name: Does NOT recommend rules for heavy content
checklist.description: Script and notes do NOT recommend keeping large additions in rules or SKILL.md inline when token costs have ballooned
